  - CREATE `metrics/archive/` directory (via .gitkeep)
- **Dependencies**: ARS-1-01

  - `zcat metrics/archive/*.gz | wc -l` equals 3500 (the archived portion)
  - Archives older than 30 days are deleted (test with `touch -t` to backdate)
  - Stale .lock files for non-existent JSONL are cleaned up
- **Estimate**: M (1-2 hours)
- **Batch**: 1.3

Ensure both are included in metrics-rotation.sh rotation policy (registry JSONL rotates, index does not).

### ARS-2-02: Create register-remediation.sh helper
- **Title**: Implement remediation registry write path
- **Description**: Create `hooks/_lib/register-remediation.sh` with the registration function. Accepts: --fingerprint, --error-type, --service, --error-pattern, --root-cause, --fix-action (JSON), --outcome (success/failure), --source. Steps: (1) generate fix_id using ULID or date-UUID fallback, (2) build JSONL entry, (3) safe_jsonl_append to registry, (4) atomic index update (flock, read, jq modify, write temp, mv), (5) confidence recalculation, (6) auto_applicable gate check (>=3 successes AND confidence >= 0.8), (7) Engram sync if conditions met (new entry, confidence delta > 0.1, auto_applicable changed).
- **Files**:
  - CREATE `hooks/_lib/register-remediation.sh`
- **Dependencies**: ARS-1-01, ARS-2-01
- **Acceptance criteria**:
  - Registering a new fix creates entry in both JSONL and index
  - Index entry has correct initial confidence (1.0 for first success, 0.0 for first failure)
  - auto_applicable remains false until 3rd success AND confidence >= 0.8
  - Recording failure decrements confidence correctly
  - Concurrent registrations (2 parallel calls) don't corrupt the index
  - Engram sync fires on new entry creation
- **Estimate**: L (2-4 hours)
- **Batch**: 2.1

### ARS-2-03: Create lookup-remediation.sh helper
- **Title**: Implement remediation registry read path (O(1) index lookup)
- **Description**: Create `hooks/_lib/lookup-remediation.sh`. Accepts: --fingerprint, --error-type (optional), --min-confidence (optional, default 0.0). Steps: (1) read remediation-index.json (no flock needed, reads are safe against atomic mv writes), (2) jq lookup by fingerprint, (3) if found, retrieve full fix_action from registry JSONL at stored line reference or re-scan, (4) return JSON with found/not-found plus fix details. Fallback: fuzzy substring match on error_pattern_summary if exact fingerprint not found.
- **Files**:
  - CREATE `hooks/_lib/lookup-remediation.sh`
- **Dependencies**: ARS-2-01, ARS-2-02 (needs data to look up)
- **Acceptance criteria**:
  - Lookup by known fingerprint returns correct fix with confidence and auto_applicable
  - Lookup by unknown fingerprint returns `{"found":false}`
  - Lookup with --min-confidence 0.8 filters out entries below threshold
  - Lookup completes in under 50ms for index with 100 entries
  - Fuzzy match returns result with `"fuzzy":true` when pattern substring matches
- **Estimate**: M (1-2 hours)
- **Batch**: 2.1

- **Description**: Create `hooks/_lib/rebuild-remediation-index.sh` that scans remediation-registry.jsonl, aggregates by error_fingerprint (summing successes/failures, computing confidence), and writes a fresh remediation-index.json. Used for recovery if index gets corrupted.

### ARS-2-07: Create auto-repair-dispatcher.sh hook
- **Title**: Implement MAPE-K dispatcher hook (PostToolUse)
- **Description**: Create `hooks/auto-repair-dispatcher.sh` that fires on PostToolUse AFTER error-learning.sh. Flow: (1) read latest pending event from repair-queue.jsonl, (2) skip if nothing new (status != pending or timestamp > 5s old), (3) dedup check (same fingerprint within 60s), (4) circuit breaker check via _lib/circuit-breaker.sh, (5) global budget check (10/hour), (6) phase gate check from agent-os.yaml project.phase, (7) lookup remediation index, (8) if known fix with auto_applicable + confidence >= 0.8: call execute-repair.sh --mode deterministic, (9) if unknown/low-confidence: in reconstruction/stabilization phases, dispatch async LLM repair via background subshell (nohup + PID file), (10) if production/maintenance: write suggestion to repair-suggestions.jsonl, (11) call register-remediation.sh with outcome, (12) update circuit breaker state, (13) write heartbeat.
- **Files**:
  - CREATE `hooks/auto-repair-dispatcher.sh`
- **Dependencies**: ARS-1-01, ARS-1-02, ARS-2-02, ARS-2-03, ARS-2-06
- **Acceptance criteria**:
  - Dispatcher processes pending repair events from queue
  - Deduplication skips same fingerprint within 60s
  - Circuit breaker OPEN causes skip with "skipped_circuit_breaker" outcome
  - Global budget exhaustion causes skip with "skipped_budget" outcome
  - Phase gate in production causes suggestion write, not execution
  - Known fix with high confidence applied deterministically (no LLM)
  - Hook completes in <10s for deterministic repairs
  - Heartbeat written on exit
- **Estimate**: L (2-4 hours)
- **Batch**: 2.2

### ARS-2-10: Update agent-os-health.sh to report repair stats
- **Title**: Add repair system health reporting to SessionStart health check
- **Description**: Extend agent-os-health.sh to report: (1) number of repairs in last 24h (from repair-outcomes.jsonl), (2) success rate, (3) open circuit breakers (from circuit-breaker.json), (4) pending repair queue depth, (5) registry size (entry_count from index). Format as part of existing health report.
- **Files**:
  - MODIFY `hooks/agent-os-health.sh`
- **Dependencies**: ARS-2-05, ARS-2-07, ARS-1-02
- **Acceptance criteria**:
  - Health report includes "Auto-Repair: X repairs (Y% success), Z open breakers"
  - Report shows pending queue depth
  - Report shows registry entry count
  - Report is accurate against actual data files
- **Estimate**: S (30-60 min)
- **Batch**: 2.3

### ARS-2-11: Create circuit-breaker-reset.sh manual helper
- **Title**: Create manual circuit breaker reset script
- **Description**: Create `scripts/circuit-breaker-reset.sh` that accepts error_type and service args, resets the specified breaker to CLOSED state (consecutive_failures=0, state=CLOSED). Also supports `--all` to reset all breakers. Uses flock for atomic state update.
- **Files**:
  - CREATE `scripts/circuit-breaker-reset.sh`
- **Dependencies**: ARS-1-02
- **Acceptance criteria**:
  - `scripts/circuit-breaker-reset.sh TEST core` resets specific breaker
  - `scripts/circuit-breaker-reset.sh --all` resets all breakers
  - State file remains valid JSON after reset
- **Estimate**: XS (< 30 min)
- **Batch**: 2.3

## Batch 2.4 — Wiring Existing Hooks (parallel with 2.3)

### ARS-2-12: Modify error-learning.sh to feed repair queue
- **Title**: Add repair-queue.jsonl write to error-learning.sh
- **Description**: After writing to error-learning.jsonl (existing behavior), also write a repair event to `metrics/repair-queue.jsonl` with status "pending" when the error severity warrants it. Severity classification: critical (INFRA, RUNTIME), high (TEST_FAILURE, BUILD_FAILURE), medium (LINT_ERROR), low (deprecation warnings — skip queue). Only queue critical and high severity errors.
- **Files**:
  - MODIFY `hooks/error-learning.sh`
- **Dependencies**: ARS-1-03 (already migrated to safe_jsonl_append), ARS-2-05
- **Acceptance criteria**:
  - A test failure error triggers a repair event in repair-queue.jsonl
  - Event has correct fingerprint, error_type, service, severity, status=pending
  - Low-severity errors (lint, deprecation) do NOT create queue entries
  - Existing error-learning.jsonl behavior unchanged
- **Estimate**: S (30-60 min)
- **Batch**: 2.4

### ARS-2-13: Modify auto-refine.sh to register fixes in registry
- **Title**: Wire auto-refine.sh successful fixes into remediation registry
- **Description**: When auto-refine.sh successfully retries and fixes an error, call register-remediation.sh to register the fix with source="auto-refine". This feeds the registry with fixes that came from the existing auto-refine mechanism before the MAPE-K loop existed.
- **Files**:
  - MODIFY `hooks/auto-refine.sh`
- **Dependencies**: ARS-2-02
- **Acceptance criteria**:
  - Successful auto-refine retry creates entry in remediation-registry.jsonl
  - Entry has source="auto-refine" and correct error fingerprint
  - Failed retries also register with outcome="failure"
  - Existing auto-refine behavior unchanged
- **Estimate**: S (30-60 min)
- **Batch**: 2.4

### ARS-2-14: Modify auto-verify.sh to trigger repair on failure
- **Title**: Wire auto-verify.sh failures into repair queue
- **Description**: When auto-verify.sh detects verification failures (status=FAIL), write a repair event to repair-queue.jsonl with error_type inferred from the failure type. This allows the MAPE-K loop to attempt repair of verification failures.
- **Files**:
  - MODIFY `hooks/auto-verify.sh`
- **Dependencies**: ARS-2-05
- **Acceptance criteria**:
  - Verification failure creates repair event in repair-queue.jsonl
  - Event severity = "high" for verification failures
  - Verification success does NOT create queue entry
  - Existing auto-verify behavior unchanged
- **Estimate**: S (30-60 min)
- **Batch**: 2.4

### ARS-2-15: Modify agent-os-health.sh to feed repair queue on failures
- **Title**: Wire health check failures into repair queue
- **Description**: When agent-os-health.sh detects an unhealthy service (Docker container down, health check fail), write a repair event to repair-queue.jsonl with error_type="INFRA", severity="critical". This is the MONITOR arm of MAPE-K for infrastructure issues.
- **Files**:
  - MODIFY `hooks/agent-os-health.sh`
- **Dependencies**: ARS-2-05
- **Acceptance criteria**:
  - Unhealthy Docker service creates repair event with error_type=INFRA, severity=critical
  - Healthy services do NOT create queue entries
  - Event includes container name as service field
- **Estimate**: S (30-60 min)
- **Batch**: 2.4

Parallel track (independent):

**Critical path duration**: S + XS + L + M + L + L + XS = ~9-15 hours

- Increment 3: 6-10 hours (deferred)

**Parallelization opportunity**: With 2 sub-agents working in parallel, Increment 1 can be completed in ~8-12 hours (Batch 1.1 serial, then 1.2/1.3/1.4 parallel). Increment 2 can be completed in ~10-16 hours (2.1 serial, 2.2 partly serial due to dispatcher deps, 2.3/2.4 parallel).

# DISCOVERY: Hook Count and JSONL Writers

Actual audit of the codebase reveals:
- **34 hooks total** (matches proposal)
- **19 hooks write to JSONL files** (not 4 as originally estimated — the proposal only listed the 4 highest-contention files)
- **15 hooks do not write JSONL** but still need heartbeat
- **17 JSONL files** in the metrics/ directory

This means the migration scope for safe_jsonl_append is ~5x larger than originally estimated in the proposal. However, each migration is formulaic (same pattern), so the per-hook cost is low.

title: Discovery: 19 hooks write JSONL (not 4 as estimated)
content:
**What**: Actual audit found 19 hooks that use bare `echo >> $FILE` to append JSONL, not 4 as stated in the proposal. The 4 in the proposal (error-learning, session-learning, kpi-trigger, skill-tracker) are the highest-contention ones, but 15 more also need safe_jsonl_append migration.
**Why**: Discovered during SDD tasks phase while building implementation checklist for auto-repair-system.
**Where**: hooks/*.sh — the full list of 19 JSONL-writing hooks: error-learning, session-learning, kpi-trigger, skill-tracker, architecture-compliance, auto-refine, auto-skill-generator, auto-verify, completeness-check, doc-sync-detector, epic-task-detector, infra-intent-detector, pre-cleanup-snapshot, resource-check, result-truncator, secret-detector, session-cleanup, tool-loop-detector, trust-score-validator.
**Learned**: The 15 remaining non-writing hooks still need heartbeat trap integration. Total migration: 19 hooks get safe_jsonl_append + heartbeat, 15 hooks get heartbeat only. Migration is formulaic but 5x larger scope than proposal estimated.

title: Replaced AGPL components: Redis->Valkey, MinIO->SeaweedFS
content:
**What**: Swapped two AGPL-licensed Docker infrastructure components for SaaS-compatible alternatives: Redis 7 -> Valkey 8 (BSD-3), MinIO -> SeaweedFS (Apache-2.0).

**Why**: AGPL license is incompatible with SaaS distribution. Valkey is a drop-in Redis replacement (same protocol, same CLI). SeaweedFS provides S3-compatible API.

**Where**:
- `docker-compose.agent-os.yml` — service definitions, depends_on, volumes, env vars
- `infra/seaweedfs/s3.json` — new file, S3 credentials config for SeaweedFS
- `agent-os.yaml` — commented infrastructure cache reference updated
- `hooks/infra-intent-detector.sh` — updated detection suggestions
- `rules/infra-intent.md` — updated storage/cache keywords

**Learned**:
- Langfuse env vars REDIS_HOST/REDIS_PORT/REDIS_AUTH must keep those names (Langfuse expects them), even though the backing service is Valkey
- SeaweedFS S3 runs on port 8333 by default; needs s3.json config for credentials
- SeaweedFS `server` command combines master+volume+filer+s3 in one process for simple setups
- Valkey healthcheck uses `valkey-cli` instead of `redis-cli`
- SeaweedFS healthcheck uses master status endpoint at /cluster/status on port 9333
- MinIO env vars LANGFUSE_MINIO_USER/PASS replaced with LANGFUSE_S3_ACCESS_KEY/SECRET_KEY for cleaner naming

title: Migrated 16 hooks to safe-jsonl.sh shared library
content:
**What**: Migrated all 16 JSONL-writing hooks to use `safe_jsonl_append` from `hooks/_lib/safe-jsonl.sh` instead of raw `echo >> file` appends.
**Why**: Centralize JSONL writes through flock-protected library for concurrency safety and hook health heartbeats.
**Where**: hooks/error-learning.sh, hooks/skill-tracker.sh, hooks/session-learning.sh, hooks/kpi-trigger.sh, hooks/auto-verify.sh, hooks/architecture-compliance.sh, hooks/auto-refine.sh, hooks/completeness-check.sh, hooks/doc-sync-detector.sh, hooks/epic-task-detector.sh, hooks/infra-intent-detector.sh, hooks/pre-cleanup-snapshot.sh, hooks/resource-check.sh, hooks/result-truncator.sh, hooks/secret-detector.sh, hooks/trust-score-validator.sh
**Learned**: tool-loop-detector.sh writes to /tmp log (not JSONL), auto-skill-generator.sh writes to .log (not .jsonl), agent-checkpoint.sh writes full JSON (not JSONL append) -- all three correctly skipped. doc-sync-detector.sh had no `set -uo pipefail` so the source was added right before the first logic line.

title: Added hook health analysis to agent-os-health.sh
content:
**What**: Added section 7 (HookHealth) to agent-os-health.sh that reads hook-health.jsonl heartbeats and reports errors/stale hooks in the summary line.
**Why**: Hooks now emit heartbeats via safe-jsonl.sh; the health monitor needed to surface hook failures and stale hooks.
**Where**: hooks/agent-os-health.sh (lines 91-153 for the check, lines 196-197 for summary output)
**Learned**: Heartbeats always go to .agent-os/metrics/hook-health.jsonl (not session-scoped). The python3 inline script parses all entries, counts exit_code!=0 in last 24h, and identifies hooks with no recent heartbeat. Sections renumbered 8-12 (were 7-11).

title: Created Remediation Registry foundation (ARS-1-03)
content:
**What**: Created `hooks/_lib/remediation.sh` — shared library for the remediation registry that stores known error->fix mappings for auto-repair.
**Why**: Foundation for the auto-repair-system to learn from past fixes and apply them automatically to recurring errors.
**Where**: `hooks/_lib/remediation.sh`, writes to `metrics/remediation-registry.jsonl` (JSONL, one compact JSON per line), `metrics/remediation-index.json` (hash map for O(1) lookup by MD5 fingerprint), `metrics/remediation-archive.jsonl` (GC target).
**Learned**:
- JSONL entries MUST use `jq -cn` (compact + null-input) to ensure single-line output; pretty-printed JSON breaks line-number-based lookups
- The `_rem_update_index` helper takes jq --arg/--argjson flags followed by the filter as the LAST argument, then passes them correctly to jq
- Index uses line numbers (1-based) into the JSONL file for O(1) reads via `sed -n "${line}p"`
- Auto-disable triggers when success_rate < 0.3 AND total attempts >= 5
- GC archives entries with auto_applicable=false and last_applied > 30 days ago
- Sources `safe-jsonl.sh` for flock-protected writes; uses atomic write pattern (write .tmp, mv) for index updates

title: Created auto-repair-dispatcher.sh hook
content:
**What**: Created the auto-repair dispatcher hook — the brain of the auto-repair system
**Why**: PostToolUse hook that fires after error-learning.sh, decides whether to auto-repair errors
**Where**: hooks/auto-repair-dispatcher.sh
**Learned**: 
- Sources all 3 libs: safe-jsonl.sh, circuit-breaker.sh, remediation.sh
- Logic flow: classify error -> check circuit breaker -> check global budget -> phase gate -> remediation lookup -> execute (deterministic sync or LLM async)
- Phase gate matrix: reconstruction/stabilization allow full repair; production/maintenance only allow INFRA actions
- Deterministic repairs run sync (<10s timeout); LLM repairs queued to repair-queue.jsonl async
- Error types: BUILD, TEST, LINT, INFRA, RUNTIME, UNKNOWN (UNKNOWN skipped)
- execute-repair.sh lib does NOT exist yet — repair functions are inline in the hook
- LLM repair path writes to repair-queue.jsonl for async processing (no actual LLM call in hook)
- All outcomes logged to repair-outcomes.jsonl via safe_jsonl_append

title: Created execute-repair.sh — auto-repair execution engine
content:
**What**: Created the core repair execution engine at hooks/_lib/execute-repair.sh
**Why**: Central component of the auto-repair system — runs fixes in isolated git worktrees, verifies, and merges on success
**Where**: hooks/_lib/execute-repair.sh
**Learned**:
- remediation.sh has no `remediation_record_success` function — used `remediation_register` to increment times_applied instead
- No phase gate system exists yet — LLM repair uses AGENT_OS_PHASE env var with default "stabilization"
- Circuit breaker API: cb_check/cb_record_success/cb_record_failure take (error_type, service)
- remediation_record_failure takes (fingerprint) only
- Functions: repair_execute_deterministic (known fixes), repair_execute_llm (prepares context for dispatcher), repair_cleanup_worktree, repair_verify (auto-detects language from go.mod/tsconfig/package.json/Cargo.toml/setup.py)
- Worktrees created at .agent-os/repair-wt/repair-{epoch}, branches as auto-repair/repair-{epoch}
- Max 1 concurrent repair worktree enforced
- Timeouts: 120s deterministic, 300s LLM prep
- restart fix_type bypasses worktree (direct docker compose restart)

title: Hooks feed remediation registry on fix success
content:
**What**: Modified auto-refine.sh, error-learning.sh, and agent-os-health.sh to integrate with the remediation registry. Created repair-status skill.
**Why**: The remediation registry (hooks/_lib/remediation.sh) had register/lookup functions but no hooks were calling them. Now successful retries feed the registry and error-learning checks for known fixes.
**Where**: hooks/auto-refine.sh (lines 132-155), hooks/error-learning.sh (after JSONL write), hooks/agent-os-health.sh (check #13), skills/repair-status/SKILL.md (new)
**Learned**: auto-refine tracks retries via $REFINE_DIR/$TASK_FINGERPRINT.count files. A success with an existing count file means "fix after failure" — that's when to register. error-learning already has ERROR_TYPE, SERVICE, ERROR_MSG available at the JSONL write point, making the remediation_lookup call straightforward.

title: Auto-repair-system Increment 1+2 implementation complete
content:
**What**: Implemented Increments 1 and 2 of the auto-repair-system SDD change.

**Why**: Close the gap between reactive error detection and autonomous self-healing across all Agent OS layers.

**Where**:
NEW FILES:
- hooks/_lib/safe-jsonl.sh — flock-protected JSONL writes + heartbeat trap
- hooks/_lib/circuit-breaker.sh — per-error-type circuit breaker (2 strikes, 1h cooldown, 10/hr cap)
- hooks/_lib/remediation.sh — remediation registry (register, lookup, record_failure, gc)
- hooks/_lib/execute-repair.sh — worktree-isolated repair execution (deterministic + LLM paths)
- hooks/metrics-rotation.sh — JSONL rotation (5000 max, keep 2500, 30-day archive retention)
- hooks/auto-repair-dispatcher.sh — MAPE-K brain: classify → lookup → execute → learn
- rules/auto-repair.md — full rule documentation
- skills/repair-status/SKILL.md — repair system status reporting
- infra/seaweedfs/s3.json — SeaweedFS S3 credentials config

MODIFIED FILES:
- 16 hooks migrated to safe_jsonl_append (flock-protected writes)
- agent-os-health.sh — added hook health + repair system checks
- auto-refine.sh — registers successful retry fixes in remediation registry
- error-learning.sh — checks registry for known fixes on error capture
- docker-compose.agent-os.yml — Redis→Valkey (BSD-3), MinIO→SeaweedFS (Apache 2.0)
- agent-os.yaml — added auto_repair config section
- .claude/settings.local.json — wired dispatcher into PostToolUse chain
- rules/RULES-COMPACT.md — added auto-repair entry
- hooks/infra-intent-detector.sh — updated for Valkey/SeaweedFS
- rules/infra-intent.md — updated for Valkey/SeaweedFS

**Learned**:
- 19 hooks write JSONL (not 4 as estimated) — migration was formulaic but wider scope
- macOS lacks GNU flock — safe-jsonl.sh has mkdir-based fallback for portability
- Langfuse reads REDIS_HOST env var by name — kept var names, changed values only for Valkey
- SeaweedFS `server -s3 -filer` combines master+volume+filer+s3 in one process
- Circuit breaker state uses individual JSON files per error_type:service pair for lock-free reads

title: Created metrics auto-calibration system
content:
**What**: Created 3-file metrics auto-calibration system: skill, hook trigger, and contextual rule
**Why**: Static KPI thresholds (e.g., first_pass_success = 0.70) become noise when actual performance diverges. Auto-calibration keeps alerts meaningful.
**Where**: skills/metrics-calibrator/SKILL.md, hooks/metrics-calibrator-trigger.sh, rules/metrics-calibration.md
**Learned**: The system uses p10/p25/p75/p90 percentile bands to classify thresholds as too-easy/well-calibrated/too-hard. Safe changes (raising thresholds) auto-apply; risky changes (lowering) require approval. Hook triggers weekly or on 30%+ anomaly in first_pass_success_rate.

title: Created tool-discovery system
content:
**What**: Created the tool discovery system — skill, reference watchlist, and session-start hook
**Why**: Agent OS needs automated discovery of new open-source tools to keep evolving. Weekly scans of GitHub + web sources, classified by relevance, license compatibility, and integration effort.
**Where**: 
- skills/tool-discovery/SKILL.md — full protocol for scanning, classifying, and reporting
- reference/tool-watchlist.md — tracks integrated, watching, and rejected tools
- hooks/tool-discovery-trigger.sh — SessionStart hook that checks if weekly scan is due
**Learned**: 
- Metrics path uses `metrics/` at project root (not `.agent-os/metrics/`)
- License matrix covers SaaS compatibility: MIT/Apache/BSD green, GPL yellow (dev-only), AGPL/SSPL/BSL red
- Currently integrated tools: Langfuse, LiteLLM, NeMo Guardrails, Valkey, SeaweedFS, Promptfoo
- Watching: Live-SWE-agent, healing-agent, Agentless, Moatless Tools, AgentRx
- Rejected: Redis (AGPL), MinIO (AGPL), AutoCodeRover (GPL), SRepair (no license)

title: Created conversation memory system
content:
**What**: Built the conversation memory system -- Agent OS learning from its own sessions (PyPy-style self-compilation metaphor).
**Why**: Engram stores summaries but not session metadata/stats. This system captures transcript indexes and extracts knowledge patterns automatically.
**Where**: 
- hooks/conversation-capture.sh — Stop hook that indexes session metadata (errors, skills used, repairs) into .agent-os/transcripts/transcript-index.jsonl
- hooks/session-knowledge-extractor.sh — Stop hook that mines patterns: recurring errors (3+ occurrences) and skill failures, writes to metrics/knowledge-graph.jsonl
- skills/conversation-memory/SKILL.md — Skill for searching past sessions, contextual recall, and pattern mining across Engram + transcript index + session learnings + KPI history
- .gitignore — Added transcripts/ to ignored runtime data
**Learned**: Hooks run in order: conversation-capture -> session-knowledge-extractor -> session-cleanup. Knowledge extractor depends on metrics already written by other hooks (error-learning, skill-tracker). Transcript content is NOT stored (privacy) -- only metadata + Engram summaries.

title: Added auto-repair docs and updated catalog/rules-compact
content:
**What**: Created docs/auto-repair-system.md (comprehensive MAPE-K architecture doc), added 4 new skills to skills/CATALOG.md (repair-status, metrics-calibrator, conversation-memory, tool-discovery), and added metrics-calibration contextual rule to rules/RULES-COMPACT.md.
**Why**: Documenting the auto-repair system and registering new skills/rules in compact reference files.
**Where**: docs/auto-repair-system.md, skills/CATALOG.md, rules/RULES-COMPACT.md
**Learned**: The auto-repair rule was already present in RULES-COMPACT.md (always-active section, line 35). The metrics-calibration rule was missing and added at the end of the always-active section.

title: Session summary: luum-agent-os
content:
## Goal
Implement a full autonomous self-repair system for Agent OS and prepare the repo for SaaS.

## Instructions
- User wants EVERYTHING to self-heal: hooks, rules, skills, agents, squads, workflows, infra, metrics, templates, tests
- All dependencies must have SaaS-compatible licenses (MIT/Apache/BSD)
- User prefers orchestrator pattern: delegate all work to sub-agents
- User wants conversation memory (PyPy-style self-referential learning)
- User wants auto-discovery of new open-source tools
- User wants auto-doc-sync (next SDD change, not yet implemented)

## Discoveries
- 19 hooks write JSONL (not 4 as estimated) — migration was formulaic but wider scope
- macOS lacks GNU flock — safe-jsonl.sh has mkdir-based fallback
- Redis changed to AGPL tri-license in 2025 → swapped to Valkey (BSD-3)
- MinIO is AGPL → swapped to SeaweedFS (Apache 2.0)
- AutoCodeRover (GPL-3.0) and SRepair (no license) are incompatible with SaaS
- settings.local.json is globally gitignored by Claude Code → moved hooks to settings.json
- No open-source tool has a persistent remediation registry — this is a differentiator
- Pre-commit hook blocks project-specific terms — must genericize all examples

## Accomplished
- Full SDD cycle: explore → propose → spec → design → tasks (all saved to Engram)
- Increment 1 (Foundation): safe-jsonl.sh, circuit-breaker.sh, metrics-rotation, 16 hooks migrated to flock, Redis→Valkey, MinIO→SeaweedFS
- Increment 2 (Intelligence): remediation registry (JSONL+index), execute-repair (worktree isolation), auto-repair-dispatcher (MAPE-K brain), wired into hook chain
- Increment 3 (Evolution): metrics-calibrator skill, conversation-memory system, tool-discovery skill
- All 10 docs updated (settings.json refs, hook/rule/skill counts, MAPE-K diagram)
- Created docs/auto-repair-system.md with full architecture documentation
- Git history wiped clean — single commit on main, force pushed

## Next Steps
- /sdd-new auto-doc-sync — make documentation self-healing (MAPE-K for docs)
- Run test suite to validate everything works
- Run /arena benchmark against BMAD and other competitors
- Start with auto_repair.enabled: false in a real project, observe logs, then enable incrementally
- Integrate Firecrawl for richer tool discovery web scraping

## Relevant Files
- hooks/_lib/safe-jsonl.sh — flock-protected JSONL writes + heartbeat
- hooks/_lib/circuit-breaker.sh — per error:service circuit breaker
- hooks/_lib/remediation.sh — remediation registry (register, lookup, gc)
- hooks/_lib/execute-repair.sh — worktree-isolated repair execution
- hooks/auto-repair-dispatcher.sh — MAPE-K brain
- .claude/settings.json — hook registration (moved from settings.local.json)
- docker-compose.agent-os.yml — Valkey + SeaweedFS
- docs/auto-repair-system.md — full architecture documentation
- skills/metrics-calibrator/SKILL.md — KPI auto-calibration
- skills/conversation-memory/SKILL.md — session recall + patterns
- skills/tool-discovery/SKILL.md — GitHub tool scanning
- reference/tool-watchlist.md — tracked + watching + rejected tools

title: Security audit for public release readiness
content:
**What**: Full security audit of luum-agent-os repo (295 tracked files, single commit) for public release readiness.
**Why**: Repo about to be shared publicly, need to verify zero sensitive data remains.
**Where**: Entire repo, with focus on docker-compose.agent-os.yml, infra/seaweedfs/s3.json, docs/business/*, rules/secret-hygiene.md, docs/hooks.md, docs/execution-backends.md
**Learned**:
- CRITICAL: No LICENSE file exists — blocks legal open-source release
- CRITICAL: Paperclip admin password default `AgentOSAdmin2026!` looks like a real password
- HIGH: SeaweedFS s3.json has hardcoded credentials (agentosadmin/agentossecret)
- HIGH: Langfuse encryption key has patterned default (a1b2c3d4...)
- HIGH: docs/business/ contains full pricing strategy, revenue projections, commercial proposals
- MEDIUM: Real Luum production domains (luumco.com, co.luum.app) in docs
- MEDIUM: Real provider names (Metamap, Veritran, Argenpay, COELSA) in rules/skills
- MEDIUM: Git author exposes personal email
- Pre-commit hook successfully blocks <consumer-codename-a>/<consumer-codename-b>/<consumer-codename-c> terms from code
- All docker-compose defaults properly use ${VAR:-default} pattern
- No .env files, private keys, or real API tokens found in tracked files

title: SDD Explore: auto-doc-sync
content:
# SDD Exploration: auto-doc-sync

## Current State

### Existing Components (Monitor layer — complete)

1. **`hooks/doc-sync-detector.sh`** (PostToolUse on Edit/Write)
   - Fires on Edit/Write of source files (*.go, *.ts, *.java)
   - Skips test, mock, fixture files
   - Classifies change_type: controller, entity, config, usecase, route, hook, rule, docker
   - Maps changed files to related docs via hardcoded mapping table
   - Deduplicates entries within 60-second window
   - Appends to `.agent-os/metrics/stale-docs.jsonl`
   - Uses `safe_jsonl_append` from `_lib/safe-jsonl.sh`

2. **`rules/doc-sync.md`** — Session-end warning rule
   - Tells orchestrator to count stale entries at session end and warn user
   - Entries persist if user skips

3. **`skills/doc-sync/SKILL.md`** — Manual `/doc-sync` invocation
   - Reads stale-docs.jsonl, groups by target doc, reads source + doc files
   - Updates docs to reflect code reality, preserves structure
   - Truncates processed entries from JSONL
   - Never deletes doc content — only updates or appends

4. **`docs/automation-doc-sync.md`** — Documentation of the system

5. **`metrics/stale-docs.jsonl`** — Live data, ~130+ entries
   - Real stale entries from code changes (controllers, usecases, entities)
   - Targets: migration-audit.md, feature-parity-report.md primarily

### MAPE-K Infrastructure (reusable)

1. **`hooks/_lib/safe-jsonl.sh`** — flock-protected JSONL writes + heartbeat
2. **`hooks/_lib/circuit-breaker.sh`** — Per-error-type circuit breaker (2 failures = OPEN, 1hr cooldown, 10/hr global cap)
3. **`hooks/_lib/remediation.sh`** — Error→fix registry (lookup by fingerprint, confidence threshold)
4. **`hooks/_lib/execute-repair.sh`** — Core repair engine:
   - Worktree lifecycle (create/cleanup in `.agent-os/repair-wt/`)
   - Verification (build + test + lint per language)
   - Merge-back via cherry-pick
   - Concurrency guard (max 1 concurrent repair)
   - Metrics logging to `repair-outcomes.jsonl`
5. **`hooks/auto-repair-dispatcher.sh`** — The brain:
   - PostToolUse on Bash, reads exit_code + response
   - Classifies errors (BUILD/TEST/LINT/INFRA/RUNTIME)
   - Phase gate (reconstruction/stabilization = full auto, production/maintenance = INFRA only)
   - Remediation lookup → deterministic path, else → LLM path
   - Circuit breaker + global budget checks

### Phase Configuration (`agent-os.yaml`)

- Current phase: `reconstruction`
- Phases define behavior flags: break_existing, rewrite_over_patch, auto_remediate_architecture
- SRE section controls auto-repair enabled/disabled

## Gap Analysis — What's Missing for Auto-Doc-Sync

| MAPE-K Layer | Status | Gap |
|---|---|---|
| **Monitor** | DONE | `doc-sync-detector.sh` exists and works |
| **Analyze** | MISSING | No severity classification (minor wording vs structural vs new feature undocumented) |
| **Plan** | MISSING | No decision logic for what doc changes are needed (update section, add section, rewrite) |
| **Execute** | MISSING | No auto-update capability; only manual `/doc-sync` skill exists |
| **Knowledge** | MISSING | No tracking of which doc updates succeeded, no learning from patterns |

## Key Observations

1. **stale-docs.jsonl has no severity field** — all entries look the same regardless of whether it's a typo-level change or a new API endpoint
2. **File-to-doc mapping is hardcoded** in the shell script — not extensible without code changes
3. **No worktree isolation for doc updates** — the auto-repair system uses worktrees but doc-sync doesn't
4. **No verification for doc updates** — code repairs verify with build/test, but what's the equivalent for docs?
5. **The existing `/doc-sync` skill is LLM-driven** — it reads source and doc, then uses LLM to update. This is the right approach for Execute but needs safety rails
6. **Circuit breaker and budget patterns are directly reusable** — same _lib infrastructure
7. **The stale-docs.jsonl already has real data** (~130+ entries) — system is actively detecting staleness

## Integration Points Identified

- **Trigger**: After `doc-sync-detector.sh` flags stale docs (chain from PostToolUse)
- **Or**: Session-end batch mode (process all accumulated stale docs)
- **Or**: Pre-commit check (warn if committing code with stale docs)
- **Reuse**: circuit-breaker.sh, safe-jsonl.sh, execute-repair.sh (worktree + merge patterns)
- **New**: Severity classifier, plan generator, doc-specific verifier, outcome tracker

title: SDD Proposal: auto-doc-sync
content:
# SDD Proposal: auto-doc-sync

## Status
Proposed

## Problem Statement

Agent OS detects when code changes make documentation stale (Monitor layer exists), but relies entirely on manual human intervention to update docs. The `/doc-sync` skill must be invoked explicitly, and stale entries accumulate indefinitely in `stale-docs.jsonl` (currently 130+ entries). Documentation drift grows silently, and by the time someone runs `/doc-sync`, the gap between code and docs is so large that updates are error-prone and time-consuming.

## Proposed Change

Apply the MAPE-K self-healing pattern — already proven for code auto-repair — to documentation. Make docs self-healing by adding Analyze, Plan, Execute, and Knowledge layers on top of the existing Monitor layer.

## Scope

### In Scope
- Severity classification of stale doc entries (Analyze)
- Planning logic to determine doc update strategy (Plan)
- Autonomous doc updates in worktree isolation (Execute)
- Outcome tracking and pattern learning (Knowledge)
- Phase-gated governance (auto in reconstruction/stabilization, human-approved in production/maintenance)
- Enhancement of existing `/doc-sync` skill with auto mode
- Pre-commit warning hook for stale docs
- Session-end batch processing

### Out of Scope
- Rewriting the Monitor layer (doc-sync-detector.sh) — it works
- Auto-generating docs from scratch for undocumented features (that's a separate concern)
- Multi-repo doc sync
- Doc translation or localization

## Architecture Overview

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                        MAPE-K for Docs                          │
│                                                                 │
│  ┌──────────┐   ┌──────────┐   ┌──────┐   ┌─────────┐         │
│  │ Monitor  │──>│ Analyze  │──>│ Plan │──>│ Execute │         │
│  │(existing)│   │(NEW)     │   │(NEW) │   │(NEW)    │         │
│  └──────────┘   └──────────┘   └──────┘   └────┬────┘         │
│  doc-sync-      classify       determine    update docs         │
│  detector.sh    severity       strategy     in worktree         │
│                                                  │              │
│                 ┌───────────┐                     │              │
│                 │ Knowledge │<────────────────────┘              │
│                 │ (NEW)     │                                    │
│                 └───────────┘                                    │
│                 track outcomes                                   │
│                 learn patterns                                   │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Key Design Decisions

### D1: Reuse execute-repair.sh infrastructure
**Decision**: Reuse worktree lifecycle, merge-back, and concurrency patterns from execute-repair.sh rather than building parallel infrastructure.
**Trade-off**: Couples doc-sync to auto-repair infra (+code reuse, -independent evolution). Acceptable because the patterns are generic.

### D2: Batch mode over real-time
**Decision**: Process stale docs in batches (session-end or explicit trigger), not immediately on detection.
**Trade-off**: Docs stay stale slightly longer (+efficiency, avoids thrashing during rapid edits, -immediate freshness). Dedup window in detector already handles rapid edits.

### D3: Phase-gated autonomy
**Decision**: Auto-update in reconstruction/stabilization. Suggest-only in production/maintenance.
**Trade-off**: Same pattern as code auto-repair. Prevents unreviewed doc changes in stable systems.

### D4: Doc-specific verification
**Decision**: Verify doc updates via structural coherence check (headings preserved, no content deletion, markdown valid) rather than build/test.
**Trade-off**: Weaker verification than code (+fast, no build needed, -can't verify semantic accuracy). Mitigated by diff review.

### D5: Configurable file-to-doc mapping
**Decision**: Move the hardcoded mapping from shell script to `agent-os.yaml` for extensibility.
**Trade-off**: YAML config is easier to edit but adds a parsing step. Use current hardcoded mapping as fallback.

## Risks

| Risk | Likelihood | Impact | Mitigation |
|------|-----------|--------|------------|
| LLM generates incorrect doc content | Medium | Medium | Worktree isolation + diff review + coherence check |
| Thrashing on frequently-changed files | Low | Low | Batch mode + dedup window |
| Doc updates conflict with manual edits | Low | Medium | Worktree + cherry-pick merge pattern |
| Excessive token usage from reading large docs | Medium | Medium | Read only changed sections, cap doc size |
| Circuit breaker trips on doc-type errors | Low | Low | Separate circuit breaker key for doc-sync |

## Increments

### Increment 1: Foundation (Analyze + Plan)
- Severity classifier for stale entries
- Plan generator (what to update in each doc)
- Enhanced stale-docs.jsonl schema with severity + plan fields
- Config: doc-to-file mapping in agent-os.yaml

### Increment 2: Execution (Execute + Knowledge)
- `hooks/auto-doc-sync.sh` — PostToolUse hook or session-end trigger
- Worktree-isolated doc updates via enhanced `/doc-sync` skill
- Coherence verification (structural + diff review)
- Outcome tracking in `metrics/doc-sync-outcomes.jsonl`

### Increment 3: Governance + Integration
- `rules/auto-doc-sync.md` — governance rule
- Pre-commit stale doc warning
- Phase-gated behavior
- Session-end report with stale doc count + auto-update summary

## Success Criteria

1. Stale docs are auto-updated within the same session they're detected (reconstruction/stabilization phases)
2. Zero incorrect doc content merged (verified by coherence check)
3. Circuit breaker prevents runaway doc updates
4. Stale doc count trends toward zero over sessions
5. `/doc-sync` skill works in both manual and auto mode

title: SDD Spec: auto-doc-sync
content:
# SDD Specification: auto-doc-sync

## Status
Proposed

## Overview

Delta specification for the auto-doc-sync change. Adds MAPE-K Analyze/Plan/Execute/Knowledge layers to the existing doc-sync Monitor. Three increments: Foundation, Execution, Governance.

---

## 1. Data Models

### 1.1 Enhanced Stale Entry (stale-docs.jsonl)

```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-22T13:54:22Z",
  "changed_file": "backend-go/apps/admin/internal/domain/entities/admin.go",
  "stale_docs": ["docs/backend-go/migration-audit.md"],
  "change_type": "entity",
  "severity": "structural",
  "plan": {
    "action": "update_section",
    "target_section": "## Entity Schema",
    "reason": "Entity fields changed"
  },
  "status": "pending"
}
```

New fields (added by Analyze/Plan, optional for backward compat):
- `severity`: `minor` | `structural` | `new_feature` | `unknown`
- `plan`: object with `action`, `target_section`, `reason`
- `status`: `pending` | `processing` | `completed` | `failed` | `skipped`

### 1.2 Doc Sync Outcome (doc-sync-outcomes.jsonl)

```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-23T10:00:00Z",
  "timestamp_epoch": 1711184400,
  "doc_file": "docs/backend-go/migration-audit.md",
  "source_files": ["backend-go/apps/admin/internal/domain/entities/admin.go"],
  "severity": "structural",
  "action": "update_section",
  "outcome": "success",
  "duration_ms": 3200,
  "diff_lines_added": 5,
  "diff_lines_removed": 2,
  "verification": {
    "headings_preserved": true,
    "no_content_deleted": true,
    "markdown_valid": true
  },
  "mode": "auto",
  "phase": "reconstruction"
}
```

### 1.3 Doc Mapping Config (agent-os.yaml addition)

```yaml
doc_sync:
  enabled: true
  auto_update: true
  batch_trigger: session_end
  mappings:
    - pattern: "*/infrastructure/controllers/*"
      docs: ["docs/backend-go/migration-audit.md", "docs/backend-go/feature-parity-report.md"]
      severity_hint: structural
    - pattern: "*/domain/entities/*"
      docs: ["docs/backend-go/migration-audit.md"]
      severity_hint: structural
    - pattern: "*/config/*"
      docs: ["docs/setup/"]
      severity_hint: minor
    - pattern: ".agent-os/hooks/*"
      docs: [".agent-os/docs/hooks.md", ".agent-os/docs/overview.md"]
      severity_hint: structural
    - pattern: ".agent-os/rules/*"
      docs: [".agent-os/docs/rules.md", ".agent-os/docs/overview.md"]
      severity_hint: minor
    - pattern: "docker-compose*.yml"
      docs: ["docs/setup/docker-architecture.md"]
      severity_hint: structural
  verification:
    require_headings_preserved: true
    require_no_content_deletion: true
    require_markdown_valid: true
    max_diff_lines: 200
```

---

## 2. Interfaces

### 2.1 Severity Classifier (Analyze)

**Input**: A stale entry from stale-docs.jsonl + the diff of the changed source file
**Output**: severity enum + confidence score

Classification rules:
| Severity | Criteria | Example |
|---|---|---|
| `minor` | Comment changes, formatting, internal refactor with no public API change | Renamed private variable |
| `structural` | API signature changed, entity fields added/removed, config keys changed | New endpoint added to controller |
| `new_feature` | New file in a mapped directory with no corresponding doc section | New usecase file created |
| `unknown` | Cannot determine from heuristics alone | Complex refactor across multiple files |

Heuristic classification (no LLM needed):
- `new_feature`: changed_file does not appear in the target doc
- `structural`: diff contains function signature changes, struct field changes, route additions
- `minor`: diff is <10 lines, no signature changes
- `unknown`: fallback

### 2.2 Plan Generator (Plan)

**Input**: severity + changed_file + stale_doc + current doc content (section headers only)
**Output**: plan object

Plan actions:
| Action | When | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `update_section` | Existing section covers the changed entity | Update specific section content |
| `add_section` | New feature with no matching section | Add new section to doc |
| `rewrite_section` | Structural change that invalidates a section | Rewrite section from scratch |
| `flag_for_review` | Unknown severity or complex cross-cutting change | Mark for human review, don't auto-update |

### 2.3 Doc Updater (Execute)

**Input**: plan + source files + target doc
**Output**: updated doc content (applied in worktree)

Execution modes:
| Mode | Trigger | Phase |
|---|---|---|
| `auto` | Batch at session-end or on-demand | reconstruction, stabilization |
| `suggest` | Generate diff, present to user for approval | production, maintenance |
| `manual` | User invokes `/doc-sync` | any |

### 2.4 Coherence Verifier

**Input**: original doc + updated doc
**Output**: pass/fail + reasons

Checks:
1. **Headings preserved**: All H1-H4 headings from original still present (unless plan explicitly removes one)
2. **No content deletion**: Updated doc has >= 90% of original line count (allows minor cleanup)
3. **Markdown valid**: No broken links, unclosed code blocks, or malformed tables
4. **Diff size check**: Changes < max_diff_lines (default 200)

### 2.5 Enhanced `/doc-sync` Skill

New invocation modes:
```
/doc-sync              # Manual mode (existing behavior)
/doc-sync --auto       # Auto mode: classify, plan, execute, verify
/doc-sync --report     # Report mode: show stale count, severity breakdown
/doc-sync --dry-run    # Dry run: classify + plan but don't execute
```

---

## 3. Scenarios

### S1: Happy Path — Auto-update at session end (reconstruction phase)

1. Developer edits `wallet_controller.go` (adds new endpoint)
2. `doc-sync-detector.sh` fires, appends stale entry to JSONL
3. More edits happen during session (dedup prevents noise)
4. Session end triggers `auto-doc-sync.sh`
5. Analyze: classifies as `structural` (new function signature in diff)
6. Plan: `add_section` — new endpoint needs doc entry in migration-audit.md
7. Execute: creates worktree, reads source + doc, LLM updates doc, commits in worktree
8. Verify: headings preserved, no content deleted, markdown valid
9. Merge: cherry-pick to main branch
10. Knowledge: logs success to doc-sync-outcomes.jsonl, marks stale entry as completed

### S2: Production phase — suggest only

1. Same detection flow
2. Auto-doc-sync classifies and plans
3. Phase gate blocks auto-execution
4. Generates diff and presents to user: "Suggested doc update for migration-audit.md (structural change in wallet_controller.go)"
5. User approves or modifies

### S3: Verification failure

1. Auto-update removes a heading (violates coherence check)
2. Verifier returns fail: "Heading '## API Endpoints' was removed"
3. Execute rolls back worktree changes
4. Logs failure to outcomes, marks entry as `failed`
5. Circuit breaker records failure for DOC_SYNC type
6. Entry remains pending for manual `/doc-sync`

### S4: Circuit breaker trips

1. Two consecutive doc update failures for same doc
2. Circuit breaker OPENS for DOC_SYNC/migration-audit.md
3. Subsequent stale entries for that doc are skipped with reason "circuit_breaker_open"
4. After 1hr cooldown, HALF-OPEN allows one attempt
5. Success resets to CLOSED

### S5: Pre-commit warning

1. Developer runs `git commit`
2. Pre-commit hook checks `stale-docs.jsonl` for pending entries
3. If entries exist: "WARNING: 5 stale docs pending. Run `/doc-sync` or commit with --no-verify to skip."
4. Does not block commit (warning only in all phases)

---

## 4. Safety Constraints

1. **Worktree isolation**: All doc updates happen in a separate git worktree, never on the working branch directly
2. **No content deletion**: Doc updates can only add or modify content, never remove sections (unless the plan explicitly calls for it AND severity is `structural`)
3. **Circuit breaker**: Same pattern as code auto-repair — 2 consecutive failures = OPEN, 1hr cooldown
4. **Global budget**: Share the 10/hr global repair budget with code auto-repair
5. **Max diff size**: If the generated diff exceeds `max_diff_lines` (default 200), fall back to `suggest` mode regardless of phase
6. **Concurrency**: Max 1 doc update at a time (shares concurrency guard with code repairs)
7. **Phase gate**: No auto-execution in production/maintenance phases

---

## 5. Non-Functional Requirements

- **Latency**: Batch processing at session end should complete in < 60s for up to 20 stale entries
- **Token budget**: Each doc update should use < 4K input tokens (read only relevant sections, not full doc)
- **Disk**: Worktree created and cleaned up per batch, not per entry
- **Backward compatibility**: Enhanced stale-docs.jsonl entries must be readable by old code (new fields are optional)

title: SDD Design: auto-doc-sync
content:
# SDD Technical Design: auto-doc-sync

## Status
Proposed

---

## 1. Architecture Overview

### 1.1 Component Diagram

```
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                          Agent OS Runtime                                     │
│                                                                              │
│  PostToolUse (Edit/Write)          Session End / On-Demand                   │
│         │                                   │                                │
│         ▼                                   ▼                                │
│  ┌──────────────────┐              ┌──────────────────┐                     │
│  │ doc-sync-detector│              │  auto-doc-sync   │                     │
│  │      .sh         │              │      .sh         │                     │
│  │  (MONITOR)       │              │  (ORCHESTRATOR)  │                     │
│  └───────┬──────────┘              └───────┬──────────┘                     │
│          │                                  │                                │
│          │ append                           │ read + process                 │
│          ▼                                  ▼                                │
│  ┌──────────────────┐     ┌─────────────────────────────────┐              │
│  │ stale-docs.jsonl │────>│         _lib/doc-sync.sh        │              │
│  └──────────────────┘     │                                 │              │
│                           │  classify_severity()            │              │
│                           │  generate_plan()                │              │
│                           │  verify_doc_coherence()         │              │
│                           │  log_doc_sync_outcome()         │              │
│                           └──────────┬──────────────────────┘              │
│                                      │                                      │
│                                      │ uses                                 │
│                                      ▼                                      │
│  ┌──────────────────┐  ┌────────────────────┐  ┌────────────────────┐     │
│  │ _lib/safe-jsonl  │  │ _lib/circuit-breaker│  │ _lib/execute-repair│     │
│  │     .sh          │  │        .sh          │  │    .sh (worktree)  │     │
│  └──────────────────┘  └────────────────────┘  └────────────────────┘     │
│                                                                              │
│  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────┐                           │
│  │           skills/doc-sync/SKILL.md           │                           │
│  │  Enhanced: --auto, --report, --dry-run modes │                           │
│  └──────────────────────────────────────────────┘                           │
│                                                                              │
│  ┌───────────────────────────────┐  ┌────────────────────────────┐         │
│  │  rules/auto-doc-sync.md      │  │  metrics/doc-sync-outcomes │         │
│  │  (governance)                │  │         .jsonl             │         │
│  └───────────────────────────────┘  └────────────────────────────┘         │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### 1.2 Data Flow

```
Edit source file
      │
      ▼
doc-sync-detector.sh ──> stale-docs.jsonl (append entry)
      │
      │ ... (more edits during session) ...
      │
      ▼
Session End / /doc-sync --auto
      │
      ▼
auto-doc-sync.sh reads stale-docs.jsonl
      │
      ▼
For each stale entry batch (grouped by target doc):
      │
      ├─► classify_severity() ─── heuristic classification
      │         │
      │         ▼
      ├─► generate_plan() ─── determine action + target section
      │         │
      │         ▼
      ├─► Phase gate check ─── auto vs suggest vs skip
      │         │
      │    [auto allowed?]
      │     yes │    no
      │         │     └──► Generate diff, present to user
      │         ▼
      ├─► Create worktree (reuse execute-repair.sh patterns)
      │         │
      │         ▼
      ├─► LLM updates doc in worktree (via enhanced /doc-sync skill)
      │         │
      │         ▼
      ├─► verify_doc_coherence() ─── headings, no deletions, markdown valid
      │         │
      │    [pass?]
      │     yes │    no
      │         │     └──► Rollback, log failure, CB record
      │         ▼
      ├─► Cherry-pick merge to main
      │         │
      │         ▼
      └─► log_doc_sync_outcome() ─── metrics + update stale entry status
```

---

## 2. Component Design

### 2.1 `hooks/_lib/doc-sync.sh` — Shared Library (NEW)

Core functions for the Analyze and Plan layers, plus verification.

```bash
# ─── Severity Classification (Analyze) ───────────────────────────
# classify_severity <changed_file> <change_type> <stale_doc> <project_dir>
# Returns: minor | structural | new_feature | unknown
#
# Heuristic rules (no LLM):
#   1. If changed_file is NEW (not in git log): new_feature
#   2. If diff contains func/method/struct signature changes: structural  
#   3. If diff < 10 lines AND no signature changes: minor
#   4. Fallback: unknown

# ─── Plan Generation (Plan) ──────────────────────────────────────
# generate_plan <severity> <changed_file> <change_type> <stale_doc> <project_dir>
# Returns: JSON plan object {action, target_section, reason}
#
# Rules:
#   severity=minor       → action=update_section (find nearest matching section)
#   severity=structural  → action=rewrite_section
#   severity=new_feature → action=add_section
#   severity=unknown     → action=flag_for_review

# ─── Doc Coherence Verification ──────────────────────────────────
# verify_doc_coherence <original_doc_path> <updated_doc_path>
# Returns: 0=pass, 1=fail (writes reasons to stdout)
#
# Checks:
#   1. All H1-H4 headings from original exist in updated (grep ^#{1,4})
#   2. Updated line count >= 90% of original
#   3. No unclosed code blocks (count ``` occurrences, must be even)
#   4. Diff line count < max_diff_lines (from config)

# ─── Outcome Logging ─────────────────────────────────────────────
# log_doc_sync_outcome <doc_file> <source_files> <severity> <action> 
#                      <outcome> <duration_ms> <diff_stats> <mode> <phase>
# Appends to metrics/doc-sync-outcomes.jsonl
```

### 2.2 `hooks/auto-doc-sync.sh` — Hook (NEW)

Trigger: PostToolUse hook OR called from session-end rule.

**Strategy**: This hook does NOT fire on every Edit/Write (that's doc-sync-detector.sh's job). Instead, it fires on a different trigger — either:
- **Option A**: Registered as a Notification hook on `session_end` event
- **Option B**: Called explicitly by the session-end rule logic in the orchestrator
- **Option C**: Triggered by a threshold check (e.g., when stale count > N)

**Recommended**: Option B — the `auto-doc-sync` rule tells the orchestrator to invoke the skill in auto mode at session end. The hook is a thin wrapper that checks config and invokes the skill.

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# auto-doc-sync.sh — Batch process stale docs using MAPE-K pattern
#
# Can be invoked:
#   1. By the orchestrator at session end (rule-triggered)
#   2. By /doc-sync --auto (skill-triggered)
#   3. Manually: bash hooks/auto-doc-sync.sh

set -uo pipefail

_HOOK_NAME="auto-doc-sync"
source "$(dirname "$0")/_lib/safe-jsonl.sh"
source "$(dirname "$0")/_lib/circuit-breaker.sh"
source "$(dirname "$0")/_lib/doc-sync.sh"

# Read config: is auto-doc-sync enabled?
# Read phase: determine execution mode (auto vs suggest)
# Read stale-docs.jsonl: get pending entries
# Group by target doc
# For each group:
#   1. classify_severity for each entry
#   2. generate_plan for each entry  
#   3. Check circuit breaker for DOC_SYNC/<doc_file>
#   4. Check phase gate
#   5. If auto allowed:
#      a. Create worktree (via execute-repair.sh patterns)
#      b. Copy doc to worktree
#      c. Invoke LLM to update doc (via /doc-sync skill in auto mode)
#      d. verify_doc_coherence
#      e. If pass: merge back, log success
#      f. If fail: rollback, log failure, CB record
#   6. If suggest only:
#      a. Generate diff preview
#      b. Output suggestion to stdout
# Update stale-docs.jsonl entry statuses
# Output batch summary
```

### 2.3 Enhanced `skills/doc-sync/SKILL.md`

Add new sections to existing skill:

```markdown
## Mode: Auto (--auto flag)

When invoked with --auto:
1. Read stale-docs.jsonl entries with status=pending
2. For each entry, the plan is already attached (from auto-doc-sync.sh)
3. Read ONLY the relevant section of the target doc (guided by plan.target_section)
4. Read the changed source file
5. Generate updated content for that section only
6. Write updated content back
7. Do NOT truncate stale-docs.jsonl (caller handles status updates)

## Mode: Report (--report flag)

Output stale doc statistics:
- Total pending entries
- Breakdown by severity
- Breakdown by target doc
- Breakdown by change_type
- Last auto-sync timestamp and outcome

## Mode: Dry Run (--dry-run flag)

Run Analyze + Plan phases only:
- Classify severity for all pending entries
- Generate plans
- Output plans without executing
- Useful for previewing what auto-sync would do
```

### 2.4 `rules/auto-doc-sync.md` — Governance Rule (NEW)

```markdown
# Auto Doc Sync Rule

## Trigger
At session end, if `.agent-os/metrics/stale-docs.jsonl` has pending entries.

## Behavior by Phase

| Phase | Action |
|---|---|
| reconstruction | Auto-update: invoke `/doc-sync --auto` silently |
| stabilization | Auto-update with summary: invoke `/doc-sync --auto`, show diff summary |
| production | Suggest only: invoke `/doc-sync --dry-run`, show plan to user |
| maintenance | Suggest only: same as production |

## Pre-Commit Integration

When the agent is about to commit code:
1. Check stale-docs.jsonl for pending entries related to files in the commit
2. If found: "WARNING: N stale docs related to this commit. Consider running /doc-sync first."
3. Do not block the commit.

## Circuit Breaker

If auto-doc-sync has failed 2+ times for a specific doc:
- Skip auto-update for that doc
- Report: "Auto-doc-sync circuit breaker OPEN for {doc}. Run /doc-sync manually."

## Configuration

All behavior controlled via `agent-os.yaml` under `doc_sync:` section.
```

---

## 3. File Tree

### New Files (4)

```
hooks/_lib/doc-sync.sh                     # Shared lib: classify, plan, verify, log
hooks/auto-doc-sync.sh                     # Orchestrator hook for batch doc updates
rules/auto-doc-sync.md                     # Governance rule
docs/automation-auto-doc-sync.md           # Documentation of the auto-doc-sync system
```

### Modified Files (3)

```
skills/doc-sync/SKILL.md                   # Add --auto, --report, --dry-run modes
rules/doc-sync.md                          # Reference auto-doc-sync rule for session-end
agent-os.yaml                              # Add doc_sync: configuration section
```

### New Metrics Files (auto-created at runtime)

```
metrics/doc-sync-outcomes.jsonl            # Outcome tracking for doc updates
```

---

## 4. Integration with Existing Infrastructure

### 4.1 Hook Registration

`auto-doc-sync.sh` is NOT a PostToolUse hook in the traditional sense. It's a script invoked by the orchestrator rule at session end. No need to register in `settings.local.json`.

However, for optional real-time triggering, it could be registered as a Notification hook (if supported) or invoked by the existing `doc-sync-detector.sh` when stale count exceeds a threshold.

### 4.2 Shared Library Usage

| Library | Used By | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `safe-jsonl.sh` | auto-doc-sync.sh, _lib/doc-sync.sh | JSONL writes |
| `circuit-breaker.sh` | auto-doc-sync.sh | DOC_SYNC circuit breaker |
| `execute-repair.sh` | auto-doc-sync.sh | Worktree create/cleanup/merge patterns |
| `doc-sync.sh` (NEW) | auto-doc-sync.sh, doc-sync skill | Classify, plan, verify |

### 4.3 Circuit Breaker Keys

- Error type: `DOC_SYNC`
- Service: `<doc_file_path>` (e.g., `docs/backend-go/migration-audit.md`)
- Shares global budget with code auto-repair (10/hr)

---

## 5. ADRs

### ADR-ADS-001: Batch over Real-Time Doc Updates

**Status**: Proposed

**Context**: Doc-sync-detector fires on every Edit/Write. Running doc updates on each detection would cause thrashing during rapid coding sessions (e.g., 20 edits in 5 minutes to related files).

**Decision**: Process stale docs in batches at session end (or on explicit /doc-sync --auto invocation). The detector only appends to the JSONL log. The updater reads, groups, and processes in bulk.

**Consequences**: 
- (+) Efficient: one update per doc even if 10 source files changed
- (+) Natural dedup: group all stale entries for same doc
- (+) No thrashing during rapid edits
- (-) Docs stay stale until session end (acceptable in a session-based workflow)

### ADR-ADS-002: Shared Library over Separate Executable

**Status**: Proposed

**Context**: The Analyze/Plan/Verify functions could live in a standalone script or in a shared library sourced by multiple consumers.

**Decision**: Create `hooks/_lib/doc-sync.sh` as a shared library, consistent with existing patterns (safe-jsonl.sh, circuit-breaker.sh, remediation.sh).

**Consequences**:
- (+) Consistent with existing architecture
- (+) Both the hook and the skill can source the same functions
- (-) Shell functions have limited composability (acceptable for this scope)

### ADR-ADS-003: Doc Coherence Check over Build Verification

**Status**: Proposed

**Context**: Code auto-repair verifies fixes by running build + test. Docs have no equivalent compilation step. We need a verification strategy.

**Decision**: Verify doc updates via structural coherence checks: headings preserved, no content deletion (>= 90% line count), valid markdown structure, diff size under threshold.

**Consequences**:
- (+) Fast: no build step, purely text-based checks
- (+) Catches common LLM failure modes (removing sections, generating malformed markdown)
- (-) Cannot verify semantic correctness (LLM might write wrong content that passes structural checks)
- Mitigation: Diff review in suggest mode for production/maintenance phases

title: SDD Tasks: auto-doc-sync
content:
# SDD Implementation Tasks: auto-doc-sync

## Status
Proposed

Depends on:
- Spec: `sdd/auto-doc-sync/spec`
- Design: `sdd/auto-doc-sync/design`

---

# INCREMENT 1: FOUNDATION (Analyze + Plan + Shared Library)

## Batch 1.1: Shared Library — `hooks/_lib/doc-sync.sh`

### ADS-1-01: Create `hooks/_lib/doc-sync.sh` with severity classifier

**File**: `hooks/_lib/doc-sync.sh`  
**Action**: Create new file

Implement:
- `classify_severity <changed_file> <change_type> <stale_doc> <project_dir>`
- Returns: `minor` | `structural` | `new_feature` | `unknown`
- Heuristic rules (shell only, no LLM):
  1. Check if `changed_file` is new (not in `git log --oneline -- <file>`): return `new_feature`
  2. Get diff with `git diff HEAD~1 -- <file>` (or `git show` for the last change)
  3. If diff contains `func `, `type .*struct`, `interface `, route patterns: return `structural`
  4. If diff line count < 10 and no signature changes: return `minor`
  5. Fallback: `unknown`
- Source `safe-jsonl.sh` for metrics dir resolution
- Add `_DOC_SYNC_LOADED=true` guard

**Acceptance**: Function returns correct severity for test cases: new file=new_feature, small comment change=minor, new endpoint=structural.

### ADS-1-02: Add plan generator to `hooks/_lib/doc-sync.sh`

**File**: `hooks/_lib/doc-sync.sh`  
**Action**: Append to file from ADS-1-01

Implement:
- `generate_plan <severity> <changed_file> <change_type> <stale_doc> <project_dir>`
- Returns: JSON string `{"action":"...","target_section":"...","reason":"..."}`
- Logic:
  - `minor` → `{"action":"update_section","target_section":"<auto-detect>","reason":"Minor change in <changed_file>"}`
  - `structural` → `{"action":"rewrite_section","target_section":"<auto-detect>","reason":"Structural change: <change_type> in <changed_file>"}`
  - `new_feature` → `{"action":"add_section","target_section":"<end-of-doc>","reason":"New <change_type>: <changed_file>"}`
  - `unknown` → `{"action":"flag_for_review","target_section":"","reason":"Cannot classify change in <changed_file>"}`
- `target_section` detection: grep the target doc for headings that match the `change_type` keyword (e.g., "Controller" → find heading containing "controller" or "endpoint")

**Acceptance**: Function returns valid JSON plan for each severity level.

### ADS-1-03: Add coherence verifier to `hooks/_lib/doc-sync.sh`

**File**: `hooks/_lib/doc-sync.sh`  
**Action**: Append to file

Implement:
- `verify_doc_coherence <original_doc> <updated_doc> [max_diff_lines]`
- Returns: 0=pass, 1=fail
- Outputs failure reasons to stdout
- Checks:
  1. Extract headings from original: `grep -E '^#{1,4} ' original > /tmp/orig_headings`
  2. For each heading, check it exists in updated (exact match)
  3. Count lines: `wc -l` on both files, updated >= 90% of original
  4. Count code fences: `grep -c '^\`\`\`' updated` must be even
  5. Diff size: `diff original updated | grep -c '^[<>]'` < max_diff_lines (default 200)

**Acceptance**: Returns 1 when heading removed, 1 when >10% content deleted, 0 for valid update.

### ADS-1-04: Add outcome logger to `hooks/_lib/doc-sync.sh`

**File**: `hooks/_lib/doc-sync.sh`  
**Action**: Append to file

Implement:
- `log_doc_sync_outcome <doc_file> <source_files_json> <severity> <action> <outcome> <duration_ms> <diff_added> <diff_removed> <mode> <phase>`
- Appends JSON entry to `metrics/doc-sync-outcomes.jsonl`
- Uses `safe_jsonl_append`
- Includes verification sub-object

**Acceptance**: Appends valid JSON to outcomes file with all required fields.

---

## Batch 1.2: Configuration

### ADS-1-05: Add `doc_sync` section to `agent-os.yaml`

**File**: `agent-os.yaml`  
**Action**: Add new YAML section

Add after the `sre:` section:
```yaml
doc_sync:
  enabled: true
  auto_update: true
  batch_trigger: session_end    # session_end | on_demand | threshold
  threshold_count: 10           # trigger auto-sync when stale count > N (for threshold mode)
  max_diff_lines: 200           # max diff size before falling back to suggest mode
  mappings: []                  # empty = use built-in mappings from doc-sync-detector.sh
```

**Acceptance**: `yq '.doc_sync.enabled' agent-os.yaml` returns `true`.

---

# INCREMENT 2: EXECUTION (Hook + Skill Enhancement + Verification)

## Batch 2.1: Orchestrator Hook

### ADS-2-01: Create `hooks/auto-doc-sync.sh`

**File**: `hooks/auto-doc-sync.sh`  
**Action**: Create new file

Implement the batch orchestrator:
1. Source libraries: safe-jsonl.sh, circuit-breaker.sh, doc-sync.sh
2. Read config from agent-os.yaml (doc_sync section)
3. Exit early if `doc_sync.enabled != true` or `doc_sync.auto_update != true`
4. Read phase from `agent-os.yaml`
5. Read `stale-docs.jsonl`, filter for entries with status=pending or no status field
6. Group entries by target doc file
7. For each group:
   a. Check circuit breaker: `cb_check "DOC_SYNC" "<doc_file>"`
   b. Classify severity for each entry: `classify_severity`
   c. Generate plan for each entry: `generate_plan`
   d. Determine highest severity in group (new_feature > structural > minor > unknown)
   e. Phase gate: if production/maintenance AND severity != minor → mode=suggest
   f. If mode=auto:
      - Create worktree (reuse `_repair_create_worktree` from execute-repair.sh)
      - Copy relevant doc and source files context
      - Write plan to `.doc-sync-plan.json` in worktree
      - Output instruction for LLM to update doc (via skill)
      - After LLM update: run `verify_doc_coherence`
      - If pass: merge back (`_repair_merge_back`), log success
      - If fail: rollback, log failure, `cb_record_failure`
   g. If mode=suggest:
      - Output plan + diff preview to stdout
8. Update stale-docs.jsonl entry statuses
9. Clean up worktree
10. Output batch summary

**Acceptance**: Script processes stale entries, creates worktree, and outputs plan/summary. Circuit breaker and phase gate respected.

### ADS-2-02: Enhance `skills/doc-sync/SKILL.md` with auto mode

**File**: `skills/doc-sync/SKILL.md`  
**Action**: Modify existing file

Add three new sections after existing "## Procedure":

**Auto mode** (`/doc-sync --auto`):
- Read plan from `.doc-sync-plan.json` (written by auto-doc-sync.sh)
- For each planned update:
  - Read ONLY the target section of the doc (not the full doc, guided by plan.target_section)
  - Read the changed source file
  - Generate updated section content
  - Write back to doc
- Do NOT truncate stale-docs.jsonl (caller handles)

**Report mode** (`/doc-sync --report`):
- Read stale-docs.jsonl, count entries by status
- Read doc-sync-outcomes.jsonl, show success/failure rates
- Output formatted report

**Dry-run mode** (`/doc-sync --dry-run`):
- Run classify + plan for all pending entries
- Output plans in table format
- Do not execute any updates

**Acceptance**: Skill document describes all three modes clearly with procedure steps.

---

## Batch 2.2: Governance

### ADS-2-03: Create `rules/auto-doc-sync.md`

**File**: `rules/auto-doc-sync.md`  
**Action**: Create new file

Content per design section 2.4:
- Session-end trigger behavior
- Phase-gated actions table
- Pre-commit warning protocol
- Circuit breaker reporting
- Config reference

**Acceptance**: Rule file exists with complete governance specification.

### ADS-2-04: Update `rules/doc-sync.md` to reference auto-doc-sync

**File**: `rules/doc-sync.md`  
**Action**: Modify existing file

Add section:
```markdown
## Auto Doc Sync Integration

For automatic doc synchronization, see `rules/auto-doc-sync.md`. The auto system:
- Runs at session end (configurable via `agent-os.yaml`)
- Classifies severity, generates plans, and auto-updates docs in reconstruction/stabilization
- Falls back to suggestions in production/maintenance
```

**Acceptance**: Existing rule references the new auto rule.

---

# INCREMENT 3: DOCUMENTATION + INTEGRATION POLISH

## Batch 3.1: Documentation

### ADS-3-01: Create `docs/automation-auto-doc-sync.md`

**File**: `docs/automation-auto-doc-sync.md`  
**Action**: Create new file

Document:
- System overview (MAPE-K for docs)
- Component table (hook, lib, skill, rule, metrics)
- Data flow diagram
- Configuration reference (agent-os.yaml doc_sync section)
- Phase behavior table
- Troubleshooting (circuit breaker, verification failures)
- Metrics format (stale-docs.jsonl enhanced schema, doc-sync-outcomes.jsonl)

**Acceptance**: Documentation covers all components with examples.

### ADS-3-02: Update `docs/automation-doc-sync.md` with auto-sync reference

**File**: `docs/automation-doc-sync.md`  
**Action**: Modify existing file

Add section under "System 1: Doc Sync":
```markdown
### Auto Doc Sync Extension

The doc-sync system has been extended with autonomous update capability. See [auto-doc-sync documentation](automation-auto-doc-sync.md) for the full MAPE-K architecture that adds:
- Severity classification (Analyze)
- Update planning (Plan)  
- Worktree-isolated auto-updates (Execute)
- Outcome tracking and learning (Knowledge)
```

**Acceptance**: Existing doc references the new auto-doc-sync doc.

### ADS-3-03: Update skills/CATALOG.md with auto-doc-sync

**File**: `skills/CATALOG.md`  
**Action**: Modify existing file

Add entry for enhanced doc-sync skill with new modes.

**Acceptance**: Catalog lists doc-sync with --auto, --report, --dry-run modes.

---

## Batch 3.2: Integration Testing Checklist

### ADS-3-04: Manual integration test plan

Not a code task — a checklist for the implementer to verify:

1. [ ] Edit a source file (e.g., a controller) → verify stale-docs.jsonl gets entry
2. [ ] Run `bash hooks/auto-doc-sync.sh` → verify it reads entries, classifies, plans
3. [ ] Verify worktree is created and cleaned up
4. [ ] Verify coherence check catches heading removal
5. [ ] Verify coherence check passes valid update
6. [ ] Verify circuit breaker trips after 2 failures
7. [ ] Verify phase gate blocks auto in production mode
8. [ ] Run `/doc-sync --report` → verify output format
9. [ ] Run `/doc-sync --dry-run` → verify plans output without execution
10. [ ] Change phase to production → verify suggest-only mode

---

# TASK DEPENDENCY GRAPH

```
ADS-1-01 (classify_severity)
    │
    ├─► ADS-1-02 (generate_plan) ─────┐
    │                                   │
    └─► ADS-1-03 (verify_coherence) ──┤
                                        │
ADS-1-04 (outcome logger) ────────────┤
                                        │
ADS-1-05 (agent-os.yaml config) ──────┤
                                        │
                                        ▼
                              ADS-2-01 (auto-doc-sync.sh hook)
                                        │
                              ADS-2-02 (skill enhancement) ──┐
                                        │                     │
                              ADS-2-03 (governance rule) ─────┤
                                        │                     │
                              ADS-2-04 (doc-sync.md update) ──┤
                                                               │
                                                               ▼
                                                    ADS-3-01 (auto-doc-sync docs)
                                                    ADS-3-02 (doc-sync docs update)
                                                    ADS-3-03 (catalog update)
                                                    ADS-3-04 (integration test)
```

## Effort Estimates

| Batch | Tasks | Estimated Effort | Parallelizable |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1.1 | ADS-1-01 to ADS-1-04 | Medium (shared library, 4 functions) | ADS-1-01 first, then 02-04 in parallel |
| 1.2 | ADS-1-05 | Small (YAML config) | Independent |
| 2.1 | ADS-2-01 | Large (main orchestrator hook) | Depends on Batch 1 |
| 2.2 | ADS-2-02 to ADS-2-04 | Medium (skill + rules) | Parallel after ADS-2-01 |
| 3.1 | ADS-3-01 to ADS-3-03 | Small (documentation) | All parallel |
| 3.2 | ADS-3-04 | Small (manual testing) | After all code tasks |

**Total**: 13 tasks across 3 increments, 5 batches.

title: Rewrote docs/business/ for open-source publication
content:
**What**: Rewrote the entire docs/business/ directory for public open-source consumption. Removed all pricing, revenue targets, ARR projections, and proprietary provider names.
**Why**: Preparing luum-agent-os for public open-source release. Proprietary business strategy docs were not appropriate.
**Where**: docs/business/ -- 6 files deleted and replaced with new versions, 4 files reviewed and cleaned in-place.
**Learned**: The openclaw-remaining-patterns.md had already been partially genericized (possibly by a previous session). Provider names (Veritran, Argenpay, Metamap, COELSA, Koibanx) were replaced with generic terms (payment processor, card issuer, identity verification provider, etc.). Pricing tiers in kubernetes-for-agents.md were simplified to remove dollar amounts. The open-source-design.md and portability-plan.md were already mostly clean -- only minor edits needed.

title: Security hardening for open-source release
content:
**What**: Comprehensive security hardening for public open-source release — credentials, license, and company-specific references
**Why**: Preparing luum-agent-os for public GitHub release. Needed to remove hardcoded credentials, add Apache 2.0 license, and genericize all company-specific references.
**Where**: 
- LICENSE (new file, Apache 2.0, copyright Luum Home 2026)
- docker-compose.agent-os.yml (removed default encryption key, replaced admin password default)
- infra/seaweedfs/s3.json -> s3.json.example (template with placeholders, s3.json added to .gitignore)
- 25+ docs files: replaced luumco.com, METAMAP, VERITRAN, ARGENPAY, COELSA, Koibanx, Keycloak, com.luum.app, luum-* headers, internal IPs, staging hostnames
- workflows/phases/security_check.py, workflows/go_service_pipeline.py
- SKILL-AUTO-OPTIMIZATION.md, skills/resume-tasks/SKILL.md
**Learned**: 
- The pre-commit hook in .githooks/pre-commit already blocks <consumer-codename-a>/<consumer-codename-b>/<consumer-codename-c> terms
- SeaweedFS s3.json had hardcoded access/secret keys that were also referenced in docker-compose env vars (LANGFUSE_S3_ACCESS_KEY/SECRET_KEY)
- ENCRYPTION_KEY now uses bash :? syntax to fail fast if not set, instead of a default value
- Many "Luum" references existed in docs/business/ subdirectory across case studies, roadmaps, and design docs

title: Production readiness test suite for Agent OS
content:
**What**: Created comprehensive production readiness test suite (8 test files, all passing)
**Why**: User requested test coverage for the auto-repair system libraries and hooks
**Where**: tests/unit/ (3 files), tests/integration/ (3 files), tests/infra/ (1 file), tests/run-all-tests.sh
**Learned**: 
- Hook stdin JSON must be piped from files (not echo) to avoid shell newline corruption in JSON string values
- error-learning.sh uses CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR not AGENT_OS_PROJECT_DIR for its project dir
- safe-jsonl.sh heartbeat writes pretty-printed JSON (multi-line), so `tail -1 | jq` fails; use `jq .hook file` instead
- auto-repair-dispatcher LLM repairs run in background nohup -- tests need sleep before checking output files
- docker-compose.agent-os.yml requires LANGFUSE_ENCRYPTION_KEY env var to validate config
- One hook (epic-task-detector.sh) is not executable; 4 hooks write .jsonl without sourcing safe-jsonl.sh (agent-os-health, error-pattern-detector, session-cleanup, sync-to-repo)

title: Rebrand Agent OS to Cognitive OS across documentation
content:
**What**: Rebranded the product name from "Agent OS" to "Cognitive OS" across all documentation files. Added a "Why Cognitive OS" section with an OS analogy table mapping traditional OS concepts to Cognitive OS equivalents.
**Why**: The name "Cognitive OS" is architecturally accurate -- it manages cognition (tokens, context, decisions) the way a traditional OS manages hardware (CPU, RAM, disk). The rebrand makes the product positioning clearer.
**Where**: README.md, docs/overview.md, docs/auto-repair-system.md, docs/README.md, docs/business/value-proposition.md, docs/business/executive-summary.md, docs/business/roadmap.md, docs/business/features.md, docs/business/case-study.md, skills/CATALOG.md
**Learned**: Directory paths (.agent-os/), file names (agent-os.yaml), config keys (agent-os-init), and hook names were intentionally NOT renamed -- only the product/brand name in prose text was changed. SKILL-AUTO-OPTIMIZATION.md had no "Agent OS" references to change.

title: Renamed to Cognitive OS — v1 public release ready
content:
**What**: Renamed product from "Agent OS" to "Cognitive OS". All 264 prose references across 64 .md files updated. Technical identifiers (.agent-os/, agent-os.yaml, hooks) kept as-is.

**Why**: The system manages cognition (tokens, context, decisions), not hardware.
